description: Qualitative overlay on top of the numeric scoring. Red flags are attached to proposals during scoring; 2+ flags trigger an automatic demotion regardless of numeric rank. Each flag has a rule the scorer checks, a severity, and a human-readable reason.

flags.name: Requires B2B enterprise sales
flags.description: Pricing above ~$500/month, customers are companies >50 employees, requires sales cycles of weeks to months. Solo part-time operators cannot sustain enterprise sales workflows.
flags.detection_heuristic: Look for: enterprise pricing, 'Contact Sales' CTA in competitors, procurement committees, SOC2 requirements, 30+ day evaluation periods.

flags.name: Requires regulatory compliance
flags.description: Healthcare (HIPAA), financial advice (SEC/MiFID), legal services, GDPR-heavy data processing, medical devices. These domains have licensing, audit, and liability requirements that a solo builder cannot absorb.
flags.detection_heuristic: Look for: regulated industry verticals, PII handling beyond basics, patient/medical/financial data, liability exposure.

flags.name: Category king already exists (>$10M funding)
flags.description: The category has a dominant well-funded incumbent (Calm, Descript, Notion, Superhuman, Linear, Figma). New entrants need a radical wedge or an underserved sub-niche to survive.
flags.detection_heuristic: Named incumbent with Series A+ funding, acquired by a big tech company, or dominant market share mentioned in research sources.

flags.name: Mobile-native stack mismatch
flags.description: The project requires Swift/Kotlin native mobile development, but the user's stack is web-first. React Native is tolerable but still slower than web ship.
flags.detection_heuristic: Keywords: 'iOS app', 'Android app', 'native mobile', 'App Store only'. Cross-reference against the user's profile.yaml stack section.

flags.name: No clear distribution channel
flags.description: The proposal has no platform-native store, no existing audience, no SEO wedge, no community. Pure 'build it and they will come' is the #1 killer of indie projects.
flags.detection_heuristic: Distribution Clarity factor scores 1-2 AND no specific channel mentioned in the idea description.

demotion_rule.description: If a proposal accumulates 2 or more red flags, it is moved to the bottom third of every ranking regardless of numeric score. A proposal with 1 flag still ranks on its merits but gets a visible warning in the report.
